stem: A clinical study study measures the diastolic blood pressure (mmHg) for a sample of 15 patients. The sample mean is 261.1 mmHg with a sample standard deviation of 49.8 mmHg. However, 3 of the 15 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 12 observations have a mean of 243.9 mmHg and standard deviation of 36.3 mmHg. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 261.1, 95% CI = [233.5, 288.7]. Without outliers: mean = 243.9, 95% CI = [222.9, 264.9]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [222.9, 288.7]. Point estimate: 252.5 mmHg.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [222.9, 288.7].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
